name: Santuario Nuestra Señora de Carejas — circular desde Paredes de Nava
region_intro: Hiking around Paredes De Nava, Spain, offers routes through the flat plains of the Tierra de Campos region, characterized by agricultural landscapes and significant wetlands. The area features the historic Canal de Castilla and the Laguna de la Nava, a vital wetland known for its birdlife. Trails are generally gentle, with minimal elevation changes, making them suitable for easy to moderate walks. This terrain provides opportunities for nature observation and exploring historical pathways. Best hiking trails around Paredes De Nava The most popular hiking route is Puente del Gallo y esclusas de Calahorra de Ribas — circular por el Canal de Castilla, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, offering views of its aqueducts and locks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de la Nava — circular by the viewpoints of the Special Protection Area for Birds, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail circles the important Laguna de la Nava wetland, providing birdwatching opportunities from various viewpoints. Local hikers also love the Santuario Nuestra Señora de Carejas — circular desde Paredes de Nava, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and towards a local sanctuary, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Paredes De Nava is defined by flat plains, significant wetlands, and historic canal towpaths. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily focusing on easy to moderate walks. The routes in Paredes De Nava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 11 hikers have used komoot to explore Paredes De Nava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect to find around Paredes de Nava?
A: The hiking trails around Paredes de Nava are generally easy to moderate, characterized by the flat plains of the Tierra de Campos region. You'll find routes along the historic Canal de Castilla, through agricultural landscapes, and around significant wetlands like the Laguna de la Nava. The terrain typically features minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for relaxed walks and nature observation.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Paredes de Nava?
A: Yes, Paredes de Nava offers several easy hikes perfect for beginners or those seeking a gentle stroll. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070173944/puente-del-gallo-y-esclusas-de-calahorra-de-ribas-circular-por-el-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Gallo y esclusas de Calahorra de Ribas — circular por el Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) long and follows the historic Canal de Castilla, offering pleasant views of its aqueducts and locks.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails around Paredes de Nava are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067271321/laguna-de-la-nava-circular-by-the-viewpoints-of-the-special-protection-area-for-birds" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava — circular by the viewpoints of the Special Protection Area for Birds&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.5 km) path that circles the important Laguna de la Nava wetland. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102737648/santuario-nuestra-senora-de-carejas-circular-desde-paredes-de-nava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Nuestra Señora de Carejas — circular desde Paredes de Nava&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.3-mile (10.1 km) trail.

Q: What natural features or wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Paredes de Nava is renowned for its natural beauty, particularly the Laguna de la Nava, one of Castilla y León's most important wetlands. This area is a crucial habitat for diverse bird species, making it an excellent spot for birdwatching. You can explore this natural environment on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067271321/laguna-de-la-nava-circular-by-the-viewpoints-of-the-special-protection-area-for-birds" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava circular route&lt;/a&gt;. In elevated sections, you might also encounter preserved holm oak forests, offering a contrast to the open plains.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The historic Canal de Castilla, an 18th-century engineering marvel, passes directly through Paredes de Nava, offering pleasant walking paths along its banks. You can see impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4300031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Gallo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks&lt;/a&gt;, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Triple Lock of Calahorra de Ribas&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself also boasts traditional architecture and a preserved medieval atmosphere.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Paredes de Nava?
A: The Tierra de Campos region, where Paredes de Nava is located, can experience hot summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with milder weather and beautiful seasonal changes in the landscape. These seasons are ideal for enjoying the open plains and birdwatching at Laguna de la Nava.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Paredes de Nava suitable for families with children?
A: Given the generally flat terrain and minimal elevation changes, many trails around Paredes de Nava are suitable for families. Easy routes along the Canal de Castilla, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070173944/puente-del-gallo-y-esclusas-de-calahorra-de-ribas-circular-por-el-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Gallo y esclusas de Calahorra de Ribas circular&lt;/a&gt;, provide accessible and engaging walks for all ages, with interesting historical features to discover.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially in rural and natural areas like Paredes de Nava. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially around wildlife habitats like the Laguna de la Nava, and to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Paredes de Nava?
A: The routes in Paredes de Nava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil countryside, the unique historical elements of the Canal de Castilla, and the excellent birdwatching opportunities around the Laguna de la Nava.

Q: Are there any elevated viewpoints offering panoramic views?
A: While the Tierra de Campos is largely a plain, the municipality of Paredes de Nava does include elevated areas, reaching up to 892 meters above sea level in zones like Villorido. From these higher points, hikers can enjoy expansive panoramic views across the Castilian plain, and on clear days, even catch glimpses of the Cantabrian mountains to the north.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads or in Paredes de Nava?
A: Paredes de Nava is a rural town, and parking is generally available within the town itself. For specific trailheads, it's common to find roadside parking or small designated areas, especially for popular routes like those along the Canal de Castilla or near the Laguna de la Nava. Public transport options might be limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to access the trails.
tips: The Church of Saint Eulalia is a beautiful church located in Paredes de Nava, a town in the province of Palencia, Spain. This church is known for its stunning Gothic architecture and rich history. It features stunning altarpieces, intricate carvings, and beautiful stained glass windows. The church is dedicated to Saint Eulalia, a Christian martyr.
Small oasis a short distance from Paredes de Nava surrounded by agricultural fields made up of a wide meadow and dense vegetation.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Carejas
